_meta.description: Storehouse layout reference for SSA. Maps household physical zones (pantry shelves, root cellar, freezers, garden shed, animal feed room, cleaning cabinet, etc.) to domains, grocery sections, and session templates. Supports forward planning (where to put things) and reverse generation (infer likely storage zone from text like “top pantry shelf”, “deep freezer”, or “root cellar”).

primaryUses: string
Whole grains and flours in sealed buckets or bins
Rice, beans, lentils, and pasta
Baking staples (salt, sugar, leavening, cocoa)
Canned goods and shelf-stable jars for quick meals
doNotStore: Fresh produce that prefers root cellar
High-fat nuts/seeds for very long storage (move to cooler zone if possible)
storageHints.rotationNotes: Use front-to-back rotation by date; keep most-used staples at eye level and ‘deep storage’ on upper/lower shelves.
storageHints.capacityHint: Design at least 3–5 shelf levels with labeled zones for grains, beans, baking, quick meals, and overflow.

layoutHints.shelfPattern: Eye-level: everyday meal kits; above: light items (chips, crackers); below: heavy buckets (grain, beans).

inventorySessions: Quick pantry scan: check grains, beans, and baking levels vs storehouse target.
Deep rotation session: pull older stock forward, mark anything nearing end of life.
cleaningSessions: Quarterly pantry reset: wipe shelves, remove crumbs, check for bugs, update labels.
mealPlanningSessions: Weekly ‘shop your pantry first’ session: choose meals around what is abundant.
Reverse generation: build batch-cooking sessions from overstocked grains and beans.
preservationSessions: Identify canned goods to replace this season; schedule new canning sessions.

primaryUses: Cured root crops (potatoes, carrots, beets, turnips)
Winter squash and pumpkins (depending on humidity corner)
Ferments and canned goods needing cooler storage
Apples and other long-keeping fruits (in segregated zones, away from potatoes)
doNotStore: Animal feed (to avoid pests and cross-odors)
storageHints.rotationNotes: Use seasonal rotation; log harvests in garden module and link to root cellar entries. Check regularly for spoilage and remove bad items promptly.
storageHints.capacityHint: Design shelving plus open floor/crib space for crates; dedicate separate corners for potatoes vs fruit to minimize off-flavors.

layoutHints.shelfPattern: Upper shelves for jars and ferments, mid shelves for small crates, floor or low bins for large root crops.

inventorySessions: Post-harvest intake: weigh and log root crops, assign bins, and sync to inventory & savings.
Monthly root cellar check: cull bad items, update remaining counts, and adjust meal planning to use up older crops.
cleaningSessions: Seasonal deep clean: sweep, mop, scrub shelves, sanitize bins before new harvest.
Off-season airing session: open vents/doors, check for moisture issues.
mealPlanningSessions: Winter menu planning: prioritize root-cellar items to minimize store spending.
Soup/stew batch-cooking sessions keyed to root cellar surpluses.
gardenSessions: Harvest logging sessions: auto-generate cellar placement tasks when harvests are recorded.
Planting review sessions: use last season’s cellar logs to adjust planting amounts.
preservationSessions: Ferment & pickle sessions based on surplus produce flagged in root cellar intake.
Canning sessions to convert near-end-of-life cellar crops into shelf-stable goods.

rows.label: Deep Freezer – Bulk Meats & Prepared Foods

primaryUses: Packaged lamb, goat, and chicken cuts from butchery sessions
Ground meat and sausage (including training/offal blends)
Batch-cooked meals and meal components
Stock bones and broth bricks
doNotStore: Short-term everyday items (keep those in the kitchen freezer)
storageHints.rotationNotes: Group by species and cut. Use color-coded bins or labels. Always log large batches from butchery and batch cooking. Run periodic ‘eat from freezer’ campaigns.
storageHints.capacityHint: Design bins or dividers for: lamb/goat, poultry, sausage, batch meals, stock bones, misc.

layoutHints.shelfPattern: Chest freezers: bottom = bones and least-used; middle = bulk cuts; top = frequently used batch meals and sausage.

inventorySessions: Post-butchery intake: tag each package, weigh, and log into inventory with freezer zone.
Quarterly freezer audit: list what’s older than X months and auto-generate ‘use it up’ meal sessions.
cleaningSessions: Annual defrost and deep clean session scheduled when stocks are low.
Quick spill clean session when leaks or broken packages are detected.
mealPlanningSessions: Freezer-first weekly planning: pick meals from older packs first.
Reverse generation: build sausage/batch-cooking sessions based on overstocked items.
animalSessions: Bone sorting and allocation sessions for pet treats vs stock.
Offal training sausage sessions linked to freezer inventory.
preservationSessions: Smoke & cure day: select freezer meats to move into smoking/canning workflows.
Freezer-to-canning session: convert frozen fruit or meat into shelf-stable jars.

primaryUses: Seed packets and labeled seed boxes (when climate allows)
Soil amendments (lime, compost, fertilizer) in sealed containers
Hand tools, irrigation parts, hoses, stakes, trellis components
Empty pots, trays, and garden-related preservation gear (like dehydrator accessories if stored here)
doNotStore: Delicate seeds in very hot climates (move to cooler indoor zone if necessary)
storageHints.rotationNotes: Track seed viability by year; link with planting plans and companion tables. Rotate older seeds into test beds or dense sowing first.
storageHints.capacityHint: Use vertical wall storage for tools, sealed bins for amendments, labeled boxes or binders for seeds.

layoutHints.shelfPattern: Eye-level for seeds and small tools; lower shelves or floor bins for heavy amendments.

inventorySessions: Pre-season garden inventory: count seed packets, amendments, and tools; sync to garden planner.
Post-season clean-up: log leftover seeds and supplies.
cleaningSessions: Spring shed tidy: sweep, dust, check for rust and damage.
End-of-season tool care: clean, sharpen, oil, and hang tools.
gardenSessions: Seed-starting sessions: pull required seeds and trays, log use against seed inventory.
Bed-prep sessions: match amendments and tools to planned beds.
preservationSessions: Drying herb sessions when using shed as herb-drying space (if climate allows).

primaryUses: Bagged feed (goat, sheep, poultry, etc.) on pallets/shelves
Loose minerals, salt blocks, supplements
Bedding (straw, shavings) in bales or compressed bags
Basic animal care supplies (buckets, halters, grooming tools) in sealed bins
doNotStore: Strong chemicals not related to animal care
storageHints.rotationNotes: First-in, first-out for feed; keep feed off the floor to avoid moisture and rodents. Log feed use to adjust future buying cycles.
storageHints.capacityHint: Plan for 1–3 months of feed based on herd/flock size; separate species by labeled zones.

layoutHints.shelfPattern: Heavier bagged feed on lower shelves/pallets; minerals and supplements at reachable height; bedding stacked against a dry wall.

inventorySessions: Weekly feed check: count opened and unopened bags, log remaining days of feed.
Mineral/salt check: assess loose minerals and salt blocks; generate shopping sessions if running low.
cleaningSessions: Monthly sweep and cobweb removal; check for rodent sign and seal feed.
Spill clean-up session: shovel, sweep, and discard contaminated feed.
gardenSessions: Manure-to-compost sessions if stored near feed room (link to garden module).
animalSessions: Routine animal feed scheduling sessions (SSA syncs feed usage to herd/flock tasks).
Health check sessions that ensure minerals and supplements are available.

phrases: in the barn by the feed

primaryUses: Homemade cleaning concentrates (vinegar-based, soap blends, etc.)
Commercial cleaners (surface, bathroom, floor, dish)
Laundry supplies (detergent, boosters, stain removers)
Cleaning tools (sponges, cloths, brushes) in separate bins
doNotStore: Food or food-contact items directly beside harsh chemicals
Garden or animal-only chemicals (keep separate zone)
storageHints.rotationNotes: Group by hazard level and domain (kitchen, bathroom, floors). Track concentrates separately from ready-to-use sprays. Link stock levels to cleaning schedules.
storageHints.capacityHint: Use child-safe locks. Keep regularly used items front and center; bulk refills higher or lower.

layoutHints.shelfPattern: Top shelf for harsh/rarely used chemicals (adult reach only); middle shelves for daily-use cleaners; bottom for tools and bulk refills.

inventorySessions: Monthly cleaning stock check: count concentrates, daily-use sprays, and laundry detergent vs cleaning calendar.
Reverse generation: suggest making homemade cleaners when vinegar, castile soap, and essential oils are abundant.
cleaningSessions: Whole-house cleaning day: auto-generate caddy packing list by room.
Laundry marathon day: ensure detergent and boosters are staged.
